SEARCH

Class MyClassicPriorityQueue<T extends Comparable<T>>

java.lang.Object
MyPriorityQueue<T>
MyClassicPriorityQueue<T>
Type Parameters:
T - the element type, must be Comparable
public class MyClassicPriorityQueue<T extends Comparable<T>> extends MyPriorityQueue<T>
A min-heap priority queue that extends MyPriorityQueue by maintaining a private HashMap from each element to its current index in the heap. This map enables O(log n) decreaseKey because the index of any element can be looked up in O(1) rather than scanned for linearly. bubbleUp and siftDown are inherited unchanged because they operate only through swap, which is overridden here to keep the map in sync via Java's dynamic dispatch. Assumes all inserted elements are unique (HashMap requires unique keys).
Version:
4/28
Author:
Ali Kablanbek

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class MyPriorityQueue

    heap

    Description
    boolean

    Inserts t into the heap and records its starting index in the map before bubbling up to the correct position.

    Swaps the elements at positions i and j and updates both of their map entries so the map always reflects the current heap layout.

  • Constructor Details

    • MyClassicPriorityQueue

      public MyClassicPriorityQueue()
      Constructs an empty classic priority queue.

  • Method Details

    • add

      public boolean add(T t)
      Inserts t into the heap and records its starting index in the map before bubbling up to the correct position.
      Overrides:
      add in class MyPriorityQueue<T extends Comparable<T>>
      Parameters:
      t - the element to insert
      Returns:
      true always, indicating the element was added

    • poll

      public T poll()
      Removes and returns the minimal element. The last element is moved to the root position and its map entry is updated before sifting down. The removed root is deleted from the map.
      Overrides:
      poll in class MyPriorityQueue<T extends Comparable<T>>
      Returns:
      the minimal element, or null if the queue is empty

    • swap

      protected void swap(int i, int j)
      Swaps the elements at positions i and j and updates both of their map entries so the map always reflects the current heap layout.
      Overrides:
      swap in class MyPriorityQueue<T extends Comparable<T>>
      Parameters:
      i - the first index
      j - the second index

    • decreaseKey

      public T decreaseKey(T target, T smallerValue)
      Decreases the value of target to smallerValue and returns the old value. Uses the map to locate target in O(1) then bubbles up from that index to restore the heap property. Returns null without making any changes if target is not present in the queue or if smallerValue is not strictly smaller than target.
      Parameters:
      target - the element whose value should be decreased
      smallerValue - the new smaller value to replace target with
      Returns:
      the old value of target, or null if no change was made

    • getIndexMap

      public HashMap<T,Integer> getIndexMap()
      Returns the internal index map for unit testing purposes.
      Returns:
      the HashMap mapping each element to its heap index
